title: Houses for Rent in Brooklyn Centre, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Brooklyn Centre, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Find houses for rent in Brooklyn Centre (44109), Cleveland. Rent ranges, RTA access, and Section 8-friendly homes from a local property manager. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent brooklyn centre cleveland
answerBox: Brooklyn Centre, on Cleveland's west side in ZIP code 44109 just south of the Cuyahoga Valley, offers a smaller number of rental houses. Two- and three-bedroom homes in the area typically rent from about $850 to $1,350 a month, and every home we manage in the ZIP code accepts Housing Choice Vouchers.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Brooklyn Centre
body: Brooklyn Centre sits on Cleveland's west side in **ZIP code 44109**, just south of the Cuyahoga Valley and directly adjacent to Old Brooklyn. It's a smaller residential neighborhood of early-1900s houses and duplexes along streets like Denison Avenue and Pearl Road, which forms part of its commercial spine. The neighborhood's northern edge overlooks the Cuyahoga Valley, giving some streets a view down toward the Metroparks Zoo grounds.

Housing stock in Brooklyn Centre is a mix of standalone single-family houses and smaller duplexes, generally on modest lots with detached garages or driveway parking, similar to much of Cleveland's older west-side housing stock.

We manage a smaller number of voucher-friendly rental homes in the shared 44109 area covering Brooklyn Centre and neighboring Old Brooklyn — part of our smaller west-side presence alongside a larger concentration of homes on Cleveland's east and southeast side. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) to tell us your household size and move-in timeline, and see what's currently available near Brooklyn Centre — or browse our [Old Brooklyn rental page](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-old-brooklyn-cleveland/) for the adjoining ZIP.

sections.h2: How much is rent in Brooklyn Centre?
body: Two-bedroom houses in the 44109 area generally rent from about $850 to $1,100 a month, and three-bedroom homes commonly run from roughly $1,100 to $1,350, depending on condition, size, and whether utilities are included. For citywide comparison, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report placed Cleveland's overall median asking rent at **$1,250/mo**, with 3-bedrooms averaging $1,350 — Brooklyn Centre's range sits close to that citywide figure.

RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which measures larger professionally-managed apartment buildings rather than individually owned rental houses, shows a citywide average of **$1,818** for a 2-bedroom apartment and **$2,716** for a 3-bedroom. Individually owned rental houses like the ones we manage in Brooklyn Centre typically cost less than those apartment-building averages.

rows: Common in the streets off Denison Ave
Often full two-story homes with a yard
Per unit; shares a lot with a second unit

body: Brooklyn Centre is served by RTA bus routes along Pearl Road and Denison Avenue, both connecting north toward downtown Cleveland and south toward the inner-ring suburbs. The neighborhood sits just north of the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo and the broader Big Creek valley greenspace shared with Old Brooklyn, and I-71 is a short drive away.

Downtown Cleveland is roughly a 10-minute drive northeast via Pearl Road and W. 25th Street, and the neighborhood's location just south of the Cuyahoga Valley puts it within a few minutes of both the Zoo and Metroparks trail access points.

items: **Transit:** RTA bus routes on Pearl Road and Denison Avenue
**Outdoors:** Near the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo and Big Creek valley greenspace

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers in Brooklyn Centre
body: Every home we manage in the 44109 area, including Brooklyn Centre, accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. As with the rest of Cleveland, keep in mind Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law and the City of Cleveland does not currently require landlords to accept vouchers — only a handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, Linndale) have local ordinances doing so. Apply for a voucher through [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net), and see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for more.

sections.h2: How do I apply for a house in Brooklyn Centre?
body: Applications go through our online **DoorLoop** portal, and every applicant is screened the same way — we don't offer a way around income or background verification, whether the home is in Brooklyn Centre or elsewhere in our portfolio. We do welcome vouchers and a range of income sources, and will consider references or a co-signer if your credit history is limited.

Since our Brooklyn Centre-area inventory is smaller, [book a showing](/p/book-showing) early to see what's currently open, then [apply online](/apply) when you're ready. You can also check our full list of [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) if you want to compare other neighborhoods.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Brooklyn Centre?
faq.a: Yes, though our inventory in the 44109 area (shared with Old Brooklyn) is smaller than on the east side. Every home we manage there accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a showing to check current availability.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Brooklyn Centre?
faq.a: Two-bedroom houses generally rent from about $850 to $1,100 a month, and three-bedroom homes from roughly $1,100 to $1,350. This tracks close to Cleveland's citywide median of $1,250 reported by Zumper in July 2026.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Brooklyn Centre in?
faq.a: Brooklyn Centre is in ZIP code 44109 on Cleveland's west side, just south of the Cuyahoga Valley and directly adjacent to Old Brooklyn, with which it shares the ZIP code.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage many homes in Brooklyn Centre?
faq.a: We manage a smaller number of homes in the 44109 area compared to our larger concentration on Cleveland's east and southeast sides. Every home we do manage there welcomes Housing Choice Vouchers. Book a showing to see what's currently available.

faq.q: Will I be approved for a rental in Brooklyn Centre with a voucher or limited credit history?
faq.a: We don't guarantee approval for any applicant, and every application goes through the same standard screening. We welcome Housing Choice Vouchers and will consider references or a co-signer if your credit history is thin. Book a showing and apply to find out what fits your situation.
